title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics · Language Lens: Feed Go Idempotent State Api
summary: Reading an entry mutates global source state, bulk operations partially apply silently, device updates overwrite each other, tags leak across tenants, and unread counts drift. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go idempotent state api increment in consistent personal reader-state model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Make set-state commands idempotent with explicit desired state, preconditions where needed, and stable response semantics.
Keep read, unread, starred, hidden, saved, and tag relationships scoped to principal, tenant, and entry without mutating shared content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Idempotent State Api · Theory Feed Go Idempotent State Api
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2432 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove private reader state, desired-state commands, bulk semantics, conflicts, and count repair; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
